birds.name: Lesser Flamingo
birds.description: The most numerous flamingo at Lake Manyara, known for its vibrant pink plumage. Gathers in vast flocks, feeding on algae.
birds.location: Lake Manyara National Park, Tanzania
birds.bestTime: Wet season (Nov–May), peak congregations May–July

birds.name: Carmine Bee-eater
birds.description: A stunningly beautiful bee-eater with a vibrant carmine-red body and a long, pointed bill.
birds.location: Open woodlands and savannas near rivers, sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day · often in large, noisy flocks

birds.name: Grey Crowned Crane
birds.description: A tall, elegant crane with a golden crown of stiff feathers and a black and white face.
birds.location: Wetlands and grasslands of East and Southern Africa
birds.bestTime: All day · flocks visible at dawn and dusk

birds.name: Malachite Kingfisher
birds.description: A tiny, vibrant kingfisher with a brilliant blue back and a red bill, found near water.
birds.location: Ponds, streams, and lakes throughout sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, perched on a reed or branch over water

birds.name: Pel's Fishing Owl
birds.description: A large, rufous-coloured owl with powerful talons for catching fish, active at night.
birds.location: Rivers and lakes with large trees, Central and Southern Africa
birds.bestTime: Dusk and after dark; occasionally roosting by day

birds.name: African Fish Eagle
birds.description: A striking raptor with a white head and chest, chestnut body, and a loud, distinctive ringing call often called the 'voice of Africa'.
birds.location: Lakes and rivers across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, perched on dead trees near water

birds.name: Shoebill
birds.description: A huge, prehistoric-looking stork-like bird with an enormous, shoe-shaped bill, known for standing motionless for long periods while hunting.
birds.location: Papyrus swamps of Uganda, South Sudan, and Zambia
birds.bestTime: Early morning, before it retreats deeper into papyrus

birds.name: Secretarybird
birds.description: A tall, long-legged bird of prey that hunts on foot in open grassland, famous for stomping snakes and other prey to death.
birds.location: Savanna and grasslands across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning and late afternoon while foraging on the ground

birds.name: Southern Ground Hornbill
birds.description: A large, mostly black hornbill with bare red skin on the face and throat, walking in small family groups across open savanna.
birds.location: Savanna and open woodland, Southern and East Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, foraging in family groups on the ground

birds.name: Lilac-breasted Roller
birds.description: One of Africa's most colourful birds, with a lilac breast, turquoise belly, and greenish back, often perched conspicuously on low branches.
birds.location: Savanna and open woodland, East and Southern Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, perched on exposed branches

birds.name: African Hoopoe
birds.description: A striking bird with cinnamon plumage, a crest it raises when alarmed, and boldly barred black-and-white wings, often seen probing the ground for insects.
birds.location: Open woodland and gardens across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, foraging on lawns and open ground

birds.name: Marabou Stork
birds.description: A large, ungainly stork with a bald pink head and a pendulous throat sac, often seen scavenging near carcasses or landfills.
birds.location: Wetlands, savanna, and towns across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, near carcasses, rivers, or rubbish sites

birds.name: African Jacana
birds.description: A striking wader with chestnut plumage, a blue frontal shield, and remarkably long toes that let it walk on floating vegetation.
birds.location: Freshwater lakes and lily-covered wetlands across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, walking across lily pads and floating plants

birds.name: Greater Flamingo
birds.description: The largest flamingo species, taller and paler pink than the Lesser Flamingo, often seen wading and filter-feeding alongside it.
birds.location: Rift Valley lakes and coastal wetlands across Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, wading in shallow alkaline lakes

birds.name: African Grey Hornbill
birds.description: A slender grey hornbill with a pale-based, dark-tipped bill, common in open woodland and readily seen perched or flying between trees.
birds.location: Savanna woodland across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, calling from treetops

birds.name: Little Bee-eater
birds.description: A small, brilliantly coloured bee-eater with a green back, yellow throat, and black gorget, usually seen in pairs on low perches.
birds.location: Grassland and woodland edges across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, hawking insects from low perches

birds.name: African Darter
birds.description: A slender waterbird with a long, snake-like neck and dagger-shaped bill, often seen swimming with only its head above water.
birds.location: Lakes and rivers across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, drying wings on branches after diving

birds.name: Hamerkop
birds.description: A distinctive brown wading bird with a hammer-shaped head silhouette, famous for building enormous stick nests.
birds.location: Wetlands, rivers, and dams across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, wading in shallows or perched near nests

birds.name: African Spoonbill
birds.description: An all-white wading bird with a distinctive spoon-shaped bill, used to sweep through shallow water in search of small prey.
birds.location: Shallow lakes and wetlands across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning and late afternoon, feeding in shallows

birds.name: Saddle-billed Stork
birds.description: Africa's largest stork, with a striking red-and-black bill topped by a yellow saddle-shaped shield, usually seen alone or in pairs.
birds.location: Wetlands and floodplains across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, stalking prey in shallow water

birds.name: Verreaux's Eagle-Owl
birds.description: Africa's largest owl, with distinctive pink eyelids and dark eyes, roosting quietly in large trees by day.
birds.location: Woodland and riverine forest across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Dusk and night; roosts visible by day in dense foliage

birds.name: Yellow-billed Stork
birds.description: A tall white stork with a bright yellow bill and a bare red face, often seen wading and sweeping its bill side to side in water.
birds.location: Wetlands, rivers, and lakes across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, feeding in shallow water

birds.name: African Paradise Flycatcher
birds.description: A small, agile flycatcher with a chestnut back and, in males, remarkably long streaming tail feathers during the breeding season.
birds.location: Forests and wooded gardens across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, flitting through mid-level foliage

birds.name: African Crowned Eagle
birds.description: A powerful forest eagle with a short crest and heavily barred underparts, considered one of Africa's most formidable avian predators.
birds.location: Dense forest across Central, East, and Southern Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, calling from forest canopy near nest sites

birds.name: Bateleur
birds.description: A distinctive short-tailed eagle known for its rocking, acrobatic flight style, with a bright red face and legs contrasting black plumage.
birds.location: Savanna and open woodland across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Midday, soaring on thermals over open country

birds.name: White-backed Vulture
birds.description: A large, gregarious vulture with dark plumage and a pale back visible in flight, often seen in numbers at carcasses.
birds.location: Savanna across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Midday, circling above carcasses on thermals

birds.name: Kori Bustard
birds.description: Africa's heaviest flying bird, a large ground-dwelling bustard with mottled grey-brown plumage, often seen striding through open grassland.
birds.location: Savanna and grassland, East and Southern Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning and late afternoon, walking in open grassland

birds.name: Red-billed Oxpecker
birds.description: A small brown bird often seen clinging to the backs of large mammals like buffalo and giraffe, feeding on ticks and parasites.
birds.location: Savanna, on large herbivores across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, riding on buffalo, giraffe, and antelope

birds.name: Superb Starling
birds.description: A small, dazzling starling with an iridescent blue-green back and a bright chestnut-orange belly, common and confident around camps.
birds.location: Savanna and open woodland, East Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, often around lodges and campsites

birds.name: Lappet-faced Vulture
birds.description: One of Africa's largest vultures, with a bare pink head, heavy bill, and folds of skin ('lappets') along the neck.
birds.location: Savanna and semi-desert across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Midday, dominant at carcasses over other vultures

birds.name: Egyptian Goose
birds.description: A common, boldly patterned waterfowl with a distinctive dark eye patch, often found grazing near lakes, rivers, and golf courses.
birds.location: Wetlands and grasslands throughout Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, grazing near water

birds.name: Pied Kingfisher
birds.description: A black-and-white kingfisher known for hovering over water before plunge-diving for fish, common along rivers and lakes.
birds.location: Rivers, lakes, and coastlines across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, hovering and diving over open water

birds.name: Blacksmith Lapwing
birds.description: A striking black, white, and grey plover named for its metallic 'tink-tink' alarm call resembling a hammer striking metal.
birds.location: Wetland edges and grassland across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, near water edges, calling loudly when disturbed

birds.name: Goliath Heron
birds.description: The world's largest heron, standing over a metre tall, with slate-grey and chestnut plumage, usually seen alone stalking fish in shallows.
birds.location: Wetlands, rivers, and lakes across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, standing motionless in shallow water

birds.name: African Openbill
birds.description: A dark stork with a distinctive gap between the upper and lower bill, specialised for extracting freshwater mussels and snails.
birds.location: Marshes and floodplains across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, foraging in shallow marshland

birds.name: Red-and-yellow Barbet
birds.description: A boldly patterned, ground-foraging barbet with a red face, yellow-spotted black back, and loud duetting calls between pairs.
birds.location: Dry savanna and thornbush, East Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, foraging on the ground near termite mounds

birds.name: Southern Red Bishop
birds.description: A small weaver in which breeding males turn a vivid scarlet and black, building woven nests low in reeds over water.
birds.location: Wetland reedbeds and grassland, sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, breeding males displaying over reedbeds

birds.name: African Skimmer
birds.description: A striking tern-like bird with a bright orange-red bill whose lower mandible is longer than the upper, used to skim insects and fish from the water surface.
birds.location: Sandbanks on large rivers and lakes, sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Late afternoon, skimming low over calm water

birds.name: Southern Yellow-billed Hornbill
birds.description: A common savanna hornbill with a large, curved yellow bill, often seen foraging on the ground in pairs or small groups.
birds.location: Dry savanna and woodland, Southern Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, foraging on open ground

birds.name: Black Crake
birds.description: A small, all-black rail with bright yellow bill and red legs, often seen walking on floating vegetation at the water's edge.
birds.location: Marshes and vegetated wetlands across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, walking along vegetated water edges

birds.name: Violet Turaco
birds.description: A large, glossy violet forest bird with a yellow-and-red bill and crimson wing patches, often heard before seen in the canopy.
birds.location: Forest and woodland edges, West Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, moving through the upper canopy

birds.name: Grey Go-away-bird
birds.description: A pale grey turaco relative with a shaggy crest, named for its nasal 'go-away' call heard across the bush.
birds.location: Savanna woodland, Southern and East Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, calling loudly from treetops

birds.name: Crested Francolin
birds.description: A small, streaked ground-dwelling gamebird with a short dark crest, usually seen scurrying through undergrowth in pairs.
birds.location: Dry woodland and thicket, East and Southern Africa
birds.bestTime: Early morning and dusk, foraging near cover

birds.name: Common Ostrich
birds.description: The world's largest and heaviest living bird, flightless with long powerful legs, roaming in small groups across open plains.
birds.location: Savanna and semi-arid plains across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, striding across open grassland

birds.name: Martial Eagle
birds.description: Africa's largest eagle, a powerful raptor with a dark hood, spotted underparts, and a wingspan exceeding two metres, capable of taking prey as large as small antelope.
birds.location: Savanna and open woodland across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Midday, soaring high on thermals or perched on prominent trees

birds.name: African Penguin
birds.description: A small black-and-white 'tuxedoed' seabird with a distinctive donkey-like bray, nesting in colonies along the Southern African coastline.
birds.location: Coastal colonies of Namibia and South Africa, including Boulders Beach
birds.bestTime: All day, on beaches and rocky colonies near Cape Town

birds.name: Great White Pelican
birds.description: A huge white waterbird with a pink-tinged bill and pouch, often seen in large groups on Rift Valley lakes alongside flamingos.
birds.location: Rift Valley lakes and freshwater wetlands across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, floating in groups or fishing cooperatively

birds.name: Helmeted Guineafowl
birds.description: A common, gregarious gamebird with a bare bluish head topped by a bony 'helmet' and grey plumage speckled with white spots.
birds.location: Savanna and grassland throughout sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, in noisy flocks along roadsides and open ground

birds.name: Vulturine Guineafowl
birds.description: A striking, long-necked guineafowl with cobalt-blue breast feathers, a bare vulture-like head, and a raptor-like gait, found in dry northern bush.
birds.location: Dry thornbush and semi-arid savanna, northern Kenya and Horn of Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, walking in tight flocks through dry bush

birds.name: Giant Kingfisher
birds.description: Africa's largest kingfisher, with a shaggy dark-spotted crest and chestnut breast, often seen perched quietly over rivers before a heavy plunge-dive.
birds.location: Rivers, dams, and estuaries across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, perched on branches overhanging water

birds.name: Woodland Kingfisher
birds.description: A vivid blue-and-white kingfisher with a red-and-black bill, known more for its loud, trilling call than for fishing, since it mostly hunts insects.
birds.location: Woodland and savanna across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, calling loudly from exposed perches

birds.name: Rüppell's Vulture
birds.description: A large, scaly-patterned vulture holding the record for highest confirmed flight altitude of any bird, often seen soaring high above the Serengeti plains.
birds.location: Savanna across East and West Africa
birds.bestTime: Midday, soaring at great height over open plains

birds.name: Von der Decken's Hornbill
birds.description: A slender hornbill of dry bush country, with a bold red-and-ivory bill in males, often seen visiting camps in northern Tanzania and Kenya.
birds.location: Dry savanna and thornbush, East Africa
birds.bestTime: All day, foraging near camps and open bush

birds.name: Great Blue Turaco
birds.description: Africa's largest turaco, a huge, sociable, slate-blue forest bird with a yellow-and-black bill and a shaggy crest, often seen moving through the canopy in noisy groups.
birds.location: Tropical rainforest, Uganda and Rwanda
birds.bestTime: Morning, moving through the canopy in small flocks

birds.name: Wattled Crane
birds.description: Africa's tallest crane, with a striking white neck, dark cap, and distinctive fleshy white wattles hanging below the chin, favouring undisturbed floodplains.
birds.location: Floodplains and wetlands of Zambia, Botswana, and South Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, feeding in shallow floodplain grasslands

birds.name: Rosy-throated Longclaw
birds.description: A small grassland bird resembling a meadowlark, with a bright pink-flushed throat and long hind claws, often flushed from tall grass on game drives.
birds.location: Moist grassland, East and Southern Africa including the Masai Mara
birds.bestTime: Morning, perched low in tall grass or flushed from cover

birds.name: African Harrier-Hawk
birds.description: A grey, long-legged raptor with double-jointed legs that let it reach into tree holes and weaver nests to extract chicks and eggs, an unusual hunting method among raptors.
birds.location: Woodland and savanna across sub-Saharan Africa
birds.bestTime: Morning, probing tree cavities and nests
